description: Book Club reading list with thematic tags for the Content Curation Agent Connector. The Connector queries this by theme, not by title. Personal notes to be added by Tom over time.

books.title: The Federalist Papers
books.author: Alexander Hamilton, James Madison, John Jay
books.core_idea: A republic survives not by trusting individuals but by designing structures where ambition counteracts ambition.

books.title: Frankenstein
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.core_idea: The creator's obligation does not end at the moment of creation — what you build has claims on you.

books.title: Discipline and Punish
books.author: Michel Foucault
books.core_idea: Power operates most effectively not through punishment but through the internalization of being watched.

books.title: Tenth of December
books.author: George Saunders
books.core_idea: The gap between how people present themselves and what they actually feel is where moral life happens.

books.title: Secondhand Time
books.author: Svetlana Alexievich
books.core_idea: When the system you built your identity around collapses, the human cost is measured in stories, not statistics.

books.title: The Shock Doctrine
books.author: Naomi Klein
books.core_idea: Crisis is not just endured — it is exploited by those prepared to move when others are disoriented.

books.title: H is for Hawk
books.author: Helen Macdonald
books.core_idea: The attempt to master something wild is also an attempt to master grief — and both resist control.

books.title: Bowling Alone
books.author: Robert D. Putnam
books.core_idea: Democracy depends on social bonds formed outside politics — when those bonds erode, institutions follow.

books.title: Hyperobjects: Philosophy and Ecology after the End of the World
books.author: Timothy Morton
books.core_idea: Some things are so massively distributed in time and space that they defeat human comprehension — but they are still real and demand response.

books.title: Groundwork of the Metaphysics of Morals
books.author: Immanuel Kant
books.core_idea: Act only according to principles you could will to be universal — morality is not about outcomes but about the rule behind the action.

books.title: The Revolt of the Masses
books.author: José Ortega y Gasset
books.core_idea: When the average person assumes the right to impose opinions without earning the knowledge behind them, civilization is in trouble.

books.title: The Revolt of the Black Athlete
books.author: Harry Edwards
books.core_idea: Athletic excellence does not exempt you from systemic injustice — it gives you a platform to expose it.

books.title: The City & The City
books.author: China Miéville
books.core_idea: Two cities occupy the same space but citizens are trained to unsee the other — the most powerful borders are the ones we enforce in our own minds.

books.author: George Orwell
books.core_idea: The ultimate exercise of power is not to punish dissent but to make dissent unthinkable by controlling language and memory.

books.title: Three Guineas
books.author: Virginia Woolf
books.core_idea: The institutions that wage war and the institutions that exclude women are the same institutions — the outsider's perspective is not marginal, it is essential.

books.title: The Power of Nonviolent Resistance
books.author: Mahatma Gandhi
books.core_idea: The most powerful resistance is not the refusal to comply but the willingness to suffer the consequences of refusal — moral authority that force cannot answer.

books.title: Fahrenheit 451
books.author: Ray Bradbury
books.core_idea: Books are not burned by tyrants alone — they become irrelevant when people choose entertainment over thought.

books.title: Democracy and Education
books.author: John Dewey
books.core_idea: Democracy is not just a form of government — it is a mode of associated living that must be learned through practice, not inherited through institutions.

books.title: Antigone
books.author: Sophocles
books.core_idea: When the law of the state and the law of conscience conflict, the person who chooses conscience pays the price — but the state pays a higher one.

books.title: Eichmann in Jerusalem: A Report on the Banality of Evil
books.author: Hannah Arendt
books.core_idea: The most dangerous evil is not radical but banal — ordinary people following procedures without thinking about what the procedures produce.

books.title: Ficciones (Selected Short Stories)
books.author: Jorge Luis Borges
books.core_idea: Every system of knowledge is a labyrinth — the library contains everything, which is the same as containing nothing, unless you know how to search.
books.personal_note: Especially The Library of Babel and The Garden of Forking Paths.

books.title: The New Jim Crow
books.author: Michelle Alexander
books.core_idea: The system was not broken — it was designed to produce these outcomes. Understanding that distinction is the beginning of change.

books.title: Anti-Intellectualism in American Life
books.author: Richard Hofstadter
books.core_idea: America has a recurring tension between the authority of expertise and the suspicion that expertise is itself a form of power — this tension shapes every institution.

books.title: The Death and Life of Great American Cities
books.author: Jane Jacobs
books.core_idea: Cities work not because they are planned but because they are complex — the sidewalk ballet of daily life produces order that no planner could design.

books.title: The Book of the City of Ladies
books.author: Christine de Pizan
books.core_idea: The canon is not neutral — it was constructed to exclude, and reconstruction requires building an alternative architecture of knowledge.

books.title: King Lear
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.core_idea: The moment you surrender power without understanding what held it together, you discover that loyalty was conditional and truth was the thing you punished.

books.title: On Liberty
books.author: John Stuart Mill
books.core_idea: The greatest threat to freedom is not the tyrant but the majority that silences dissent — protecting the unpopular opinion is what makes a free society function.

books.title: The Theory of the Leisure Class
books.author: Thorstein Veblen
books.core_idea: Economic behavior is driven less by utility than by the need to signal status — consumption is a performance, not a necessity.

books.title: Notes from Underground
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.core_idea: Humans will act against their own interest purely to prove they are free — rationality cannot fully account for the perversity of consciousness.

books.title: Discourse on the Origin of Inequality (On the Nature of Pity)
books.author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
books.core_idea: Pity is the natural moral sentiment that precedes reason — civilization did not create compassion, it complicated it.

books.title: Nicomachean Ethics
books.author: Aristotle
books.core_idea: Virtue is not a feeling or a theory — it is a habit built through practice. You become good by doing good, not by knowing what good is.

books.title: History of the Peloponnesian War
books.author: Thucydides
books.core_idea: The strong do what they can and the weak suffer what they must — but the strong are also destroyed by their own overreach, which they never see coming.
